stories.title: State conveys ~19,950 acres near Houston to AIDEA for data-center industrial park
stories.notes: Triple-confirmed. Primary DNR notice supports acreage, ADL, no-cost AIDEA conveyance, location, deadline. Data-center use comes from decision attachment + coverage, not the primary notice body. '60 road miles' and '41 pages' dropped as unverifiable.

stories.title: FY2027 NDAA stall freezes Alaska defense autonomy money
stories.notes: RUNNER-UP. Money items are AUTONOMY and ANALYTICS/SIMULATION, not AI. Only the $10M Golden Dome item is explicitly tied to Alaska. Must not relabel as AI.

stories.title: FAA clears Drone Amplified MONTIS avalanche-control drone; Alaska DOT partner
stories.notes: Not selected. 'Juneau' demo location dropped; 'still uses howitzers' softened to 'moving away from military artillery'. Weak AI relevance (drone autonomy).

claims.text: The DNR preliminary decision proposes conveying approximately 19,950 acres of state land in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.
claims.verification_note: Page reads 'approximately 19,950 acres (subject parcel) of State-owned land in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.' Coverage characterizes this as roughly 30 to 31 square miles.

claims.text: The parcel is roughly 31 square miles.
claims.verification_note: Coverage converts 19,950 acres to roughly 30 to 31 square miles. 19,950 acres / 640 = 31.2 sq mi, arithmetic-consistent.

claims.text: The proposed conveyance is filed under case number ADL 234762.
claims.verification_note: ADL number 234762 shown on the notice page.

claims.text: DNR proposes a noncompetitive, no-cost conveyance of the land to the Alaska Industrial Development and Export Authority.
claims.verification_note: Notice describes a 'Noncompetitive sale through public and charitable use conveyance to AIDEA'. Northern Journal and Alaska's News Source confirm 'at no cost.'

claims.text: The parcel sits approximately two miles north of Houston in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough.
claims.verification_note: Notice reads 'approximately two miles north of Houston in the Matanuska-Susitna Borough, within Township 18 North, and Township 19 North.'

claims.text: The public comment period on the proposed transfer runs through 5:00 p.m. on Wednesday, August 19, 2026.
claims.verification_note: Notice reads '5:00 PM, Wednesday, August 19, 2026.' Corroborated by Mat-Su Sentinel and Alaska's News Source.

claims.text: The proposed industrial park is planned to include scalable data centers as part of a multi-use industrial and energy development district.
claims.verification_note: Mat-Su Sentinel and Alaska's News Source both report 'scalable data centers' as a planned use. DNR primary notice references a 'Houston Industrial Park' but does not print 'data centers' in the main body; that detail is in the decision attachment and coverage.
claims.suggested_phrasing: planned to include scalable data centers, per the project decision and local coverage

claims.text: Alaska currently has 8 data centers, among the fewest of any state.
claims.verification_note: Page states current Alaska data centers '8 total (4th fewest nationally).'

claims.text: Virginia leads all states with 639 data centers.
claims.verification_note: Page states 'Virginia leads with 639 data centers.'

claims.text: Houston City Council member Lisa Johansen has proposed a ban on data centers within city limits.
claims.verification_note: Page names 'Lisa Johansen, a Houston City Council member' who 'has already proposed a ban on data centers within city limits.'

claims.text: On July 14, 2026 the Senate voted 50 to 46 on the FY2027 NDAA, failing the 60-vote threshold needed to bring it to the floor.
claims.verification_note: Page reads 'Senators voted 50-46 on party lines, failing to meet the 60-vote threshold needed to bring it to the floor.'

claims.text: The stalled FY2027 NDAA would authorize about 1.14 trillion dollars in Defense Department funding.
claims.verification_note: Page reads 'would greenlight about $1.14 trillion in funding for the Defense Department.'

claims.text: Senator Dan Sullivan secured 56 provisions and amendments for Alaska in the FY2027 defense authorization bill.
claims.verification_note: Page reads '56 provision and amendments authored by Sen. Sullivan.'

claims.text: The bill authorizes 2.6 billion dollars in military construction and projects specifically for Alaska.
claims.verification_note: Page reads '$2.6 billion in military construction and projects specifically for Alaska.'

claims.text: The bill includes 10 million dollars for autonomy within the Golden Dome missile defense system, which will include projects in Alaska.
claims.verification_note: Verbatim: '$10 million for autonomy within the Golden Dome missile defense system, which will include projects in Alaska.' AUTONOMY funding, not labeled AI.

claims.text: The bill directs establishment of an Analytics and Simulation Laboratory within the Ted Stevens Center for Arctic Security Studies.
claims.verification_note: Verbatim 'Establish an Analytics and Simulation Laboratory within the Ted Stevens Center for Arctic Security Studies.' Analytics/simulation, NOT an AI lab.

claims.text: On July 8, 2026 Drone Amplified announced the FAA granted regulatory approval for operation of its MONTIS drone-based avalanche control system.
Alaska DOT is credited as a collaborator in bringing MONTIS through the FAA approval process.
claims.verification_note: CEO Carrick Detweiler quoted referencing collaboration with the Alaska Department of Transportation.

claims.text: Alaska has long leaned on military artillery for avalanche control and is now moving to remote systems.
claims.verification_note: Page: 'The State of Alaska is moving away from military artillery as a primary means of avalanche mitigation and will adopt Remote Avalanche Control Systems (RACS).' Transition framing, not 'still uses'.

dropped.claim: The Houston data-center site is about 60 road miles north of Anchorage.
dropped.reason: Not found on any fetched page; single-sourced to scout summary. Dropped.

dropped.claim: The 2024 MONTIS demonstration took place in Juneau.
dropped.reason: Primary AK DOT case page states only 'early January' 2024; no location. Dropped.

dropped.claim: The DNR preliminary decision is 41 pages long.
dropped.reason: Not in fetched HTML; count is in the un-fetched decision PDF. Dropped.

dropped.claim: The $15M Arctic autonomous sensing and Ted Stevens analytics lab are 'AI' programs.
dropped.reason: Sources label them 'autonomous sensing' and 'Analytics and Simulation Laboratory'; reframing as AI would overclaim.
